context: C94 introduced `protected get storageKey(): string { return this.constructor.name; }` on MinigameBase. All 14 subclasses rely on the default getter. This audit covers vite build config, mg_fouille, and mg_sang_froid.

questions.title: storageKey stability under Vite/esbuild production build

findings: vite.config.ts has no `minify` field — Vite defaults to `esbuild` minifier in production.
Vite's default esbuild minification sets `minifyIdentifiers: false` for class names to preserve them (esbuild only mangles local variable names, not exported class names).
All 14 minigame classes are statically imported via MinigameRegistry.ts and therefore EXPORTED symbols — esbuild never mangles exported identifiers.
The manual chunk config splits minigames into a 'minigames' chunk but does not alter identifier preservation rules.
No terser config is present. No `build.minify: 'terser'` override. No `esbuild.minifyIdentifiers: true`.
`sourcemap: true` is set — this confirms class names must survive minification for source maps to be useful.
Result: `this.constructor.name` reliably returns e.g. 'MinigameFouille' at runtime. localStorage keys will be `merlin_mg_plays_MinigameFouille` etc. across dev and prod builds.
analysis.risk_condition: Would become HIGH if a future commit adds `build.minify: 'terser'` with aggressive options, or `esbuild: { minifyIdentifiers: true }`. No such config exists today.

questions.title: mg_fouille.ts cleanup audit

cancelTimers_or_cleanup_clears_timers.detail: cleanup() calls clearTimeout(this.foundTimeout), clearInterval(this.timerInterval), and cancelAnimationFrame(this.animFrame) at lines 439-441. No separate cancelTimers() method needed — all timer handles are cleared directly in cleanup().

endGame_double_finish_guard.detail: `endGame()` opens with `if (this.ended) return;` at line 291 then immediately sets `this.ended = true`. This is a correct double-finish guard. The onClick and onKeyDown paths both call endGame() only after setting `this.found = true`, but even without that the guard is present.

all_event_listeners_removed_in_cleanup.detail: Three listeners are added in setup() on the canvas: `pointermove` (onPointerMove), `pointerdown` (onClick), `keydown` (onKeyDown). cleanup() removes all three on the same canvas reference at lines 442-444. endGame() also removes them at lines 296-298 so they are removed at the earliest possible point. No document-scope or window-scope listeners anywhere in the file.

orphaned_setTimeouts.detail: `foundTimeout` is the only setTimeout in the file. It is stored in `this.foundTimeout`. It is cleared in both endGame() (line 293) and cleanup() (line 439). The 400ms delay fires endGame(), which sets ended=true, so a second fire is a no-op even if clearTimeout were somehow missed.

document_scope_listeners.detail: No `document.addEventListener` or `window.addEventListener` calls anywhere in mg_fouille.ts.

questions.notes: The file is clean. Minor stylistic observation only: endGame() duplicates the three removeEventListener calls that cleanup() also makes — this is harmless (removing a non-attached listener is a no-op) but results in the listeners being removed twice on a normal game-over path. Not a bug.

questions.title: mg_sang_froid.ts cleanup audit

cancelTimers_or_cleanup_clears_timers.detail: cleanup() at lines 371-379 calls clearInterval(this.timerInterval) and cancelAnimationFrame(this.animFrame). No foundTimeout equivalent exists in this minigame (no delayed endGame call).

endGame_double_finish_guard.detail: `endGame()` opens with `if (this.ended) return;` at line 186 then sets `this.ended = true`. The timer interval is the only path that can call endGame(), but the guard correctly protects against any race.

all_event_listeners_removed_in_cleanup.detail: Five listeners are added in setup(): `pointermove` (onPointerMove), `pointerdown` (onPointerMove), `pointerleave` (onPointerLeave), `pointercancel` (onPointerLeave), `keydown` (onKeyDown). endGame() at lines 190-194 removes all five. cleanup() at lines 374-378 also removes all five. No document-scope or window-scope listeners in the file.

orphaned_setTimeouts.detail: No setTimeout calls anywhere in mg_sang_froid.ts. Only setInterval (stored as this.timerInterval) and requestAnimationFrame (stored as this.animFrame).

document_scope_listeners.detail: No `document.addEventListener` or `window.addEventListener` calls in mg_sang_froid.ts. The SFX dispatches use `window.dispatchEvent` (write-only, no listener registration).

questions.notes: Same minor duplicate-removal pattern as mg_fouille: endGame() and cleanup() both remove all listeners. Harmless. The `window.dispatchEvent` calls for SFX (lines 244-247) are fire-and-forget dispatches, not listener registrations — no leak.

summary: All three questions pass with high confidence. The storageKey mechanism is safe under the current Vite/esbuild configuration. Both mg_fouille and mg_sang_froid have correct double-finish guards, full timer cleanup, and no orphaned document-scope listeners. The only structural note is a harmless double-removal pattern (endGame + cleanup both call removeEventListener) present in both files — this is defensive coding, not a bug.
